skill.name: distributed-debugging-debug-trace
skill.description: Expert in setting up debugging environments, distributed tracing, and diagnostic tools for development and production.
skill.summary: Configure debugging workflows, implement tracing solutions, and establish diagnostics for multi-service systems.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 183 patterns, but most are false positives. JavaScript template literals were misidentified as Ruby shell execution (52 findings). Environment variable access (62 findings) is standard Node.js configuration practice. One legitimate medium-severity finding: innerHTML usage in debug dashboard example code could enable XSS if WebSocket data is untrusted. Skill provides legitimate debugging and observability patterns.

medium_findings.title: Potential XSS via innerHTML
medium_findings.description: Debug dashboard example code uses innerHTML to render WebSocket data without sanitization. While this is documentation example code, users implementing this pattern should sanitize untrusted data.

content.value_statement: Struggling to debug complex multi-service systems? This skill sets up comprehensive debugging environments, distributed tracing, and diagnostic tools to help you quickly identify and resolve issues across your infrastructure.
actual_capabilities: Configure VS Code and IDE debugging for Node.js, TypeScript, and Jest
Set up distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry and Jaeger
Implement structured logging with Winston and Elasticsearch
Create performance profilers for CPU and heap analysis
Design production-safe debug dashboards with real-time metrics
Establish debug configurations for remote and containerized environments
limitations: Example code uses innerHTML which requires sanitization for production use
Debug dashboards are demonstration templates requiring security hardening
Remote debugging requires network access and should not be enabled in production without authentication
Profiling tools may impact application performance during active sessions

use_cases.title: Multi-Service Debugging Setup
use_cases.description: Configure end-to-end debugging for microservices architecture with trace correlation across service boundaries.

use_cases.title: Production Issue Diagnosis
use_cases.description: Establish safe production debugging capabilities with sampling, authentication, and redacted sensitive data.

use_cases.title: Performance Optimization
use_cases.description: Implement CPU profiling, heap snapshots, and memory leak detection for performance-critical applications.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Debug Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up VS Code debugging for my Node.js application with source maps and environment variables.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer needs to debug a local Node.js application with TypeScript support

prompt_templates.title: Distributed Tracing Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure OpenTelemetry tracing for my Express microservices with Jaeger as the backend. Include span context propagation and custom span attributes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Team needs to trace requests across multiple microservices

prompt_templates.title: Production Debug Dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production-safe debug dashboard showing memory usage, active traces, and structured logs. Include authentication and IP allowlisting.
prompt_templates.scenario: SRE team needs real-time visibility into production system health

prompt_templates.title: Memory Leak Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me investigate a memory leak in my Node.js service. Set up heap snapshot automation, memory trend monitoring, and alert thresholds.
prompt_templates.scenario: Application shows increasing memory usage over time, suspected memory leak

output_examples.input: Set up debugging for my Express API
output: Added source map support with --enable-source-maps flag
Configured environment variables for DEBUG and NODE_ENV
Set up skipFiles to exclude node_modules from stepping
Added compound configuration for full-stack debugging
Configured auto-instrumentations for HTTP and Express
Added custom span middleware with trace context propagation
Set up resource attributes for service identification
Implemented graceful shutdown with span flushing

best_practices: Always sample traces in production to avoid performance overhead and storage costs
Redact sensitive data like API keys, tokens, and PII from all logs and trace attributes
Use structured logging with consistent field names for better searchability and analysis
anti_patterns: Enabling verbose debug logging in production without rate limiting
Exposing remote debug ports without authentication or network restrictions
Using innerHTML with untrusted data in debug dashboards without sanitization

faq.question: Is remote debugging safe for production environments?
faq.answer: Remote debugging should only be enabled with strict authentication, IP allowlisting, and short timeout windows. Consider using ephemeral debug sessions triggered by specific events rather than always-on debug servers.

faq.question: How do I prevent sensitive data from appearing in traces?
faq.answer: Use span attribute filters to exclude fields containing secrets. Configure your logger to redact known sensitive patterns. Review auto-instrumentation hooks to ensure they do not capture request bodies with credentials.

faq.question: What sampling rate should I use for production tracing?
faq.answer: Start with 1-10% sampling for high-traffic services. Increase to 100% for low-traffic services or during incident investigation. Use head-based sampling for consistent trace collection across services.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with non-Node.js applications?
faq.answer: The core concepts apply to any language. OpenTelemetry has SDKs for Python, Go, Java, and other languages. Adapt the configuration patterns to your tech stack while following the same tracing principles.

faq.question: How do I correlate logs with traces?
faq.answer: Inject trace ID and span ID into your log context. Most tracing libraries provide middleware to automatically add these IDs to the request context. Ensure your logger reads from this context for each log entry.

faq.question: What is the performance impact of distributed tracing?
faq.answer: Well-configured tracing adds 1-5% overhead. Use batch span processors, adjust sampling rates, and avoid capturing large payloads in span attributes. Profile your specific application to measure actual impact.
